title: Google launches three new Gemini models: Flash 3.6, Flash-Lite 3.5 and Flash Cyber 3.5
summary: The new AI models focus on speed and cybersecurity, while the 3.5 Pro model continues without a release date.

title: Alibaba launches Qwen-Image-3.0, an image generator capable of creating infographics with readable text
summary: The AI model supports up to 4,500 input tokens and can generate complex images with small readable text in a single pass.

title: Anthropic reaches $1.5 billion settlement in copyright lawsuit
summary: The startup will pay that amount to resolve the class action lawsuit for using copyrighted books in training its AI models.

title: Nvidia introduces DLSS 5 with three AI modes and per-object control
summary: The new version of AI upscaling allows switching between models in real time and offers support for a single GPU.

title: Nvidia details its Vera CPU with 88 Olympus cores for AI data centers
summary: The CPU shows 176 threads, 1.2 TB/s LPDDR5X memory, and its performance in SPEC CPU 2026 benchmarks.

title: Chinese model Kimi K3 from Moonshot AI shakes up the industry with lower price than its US competitors
summary: The 2.8 billion parameter open model rekindles the debate over Chinese AI control and its impact on US competitiveness.

title: Apple partners with Klarna to offer a lease-to-own program for devices
summary: The program will be available from July 28 and includes iPhones, iPads, and Macs with monthly payments.

title: Google develops 'Frozen v2' chip for Gemini that could multiply energy efficiency by up to ten
summary: The new processor would be specifically designed for the Gemini architecture and projects between 6 and 10 times more tokens per watt than current TPUs.
